hostel_campus.college_name: Pondicherry Engineering College - PEC Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description: There are two boys hostels (for U.G students), one boys hostel (for P.G. students). The two boys hostels have the capacity to accommodate 270 students. The P.G. hostel has a capacity of 60 single rooms.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Badminton Court, Basketball Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground, Table Tennis, Volleyball

hostel.description: There are two boys hostels (for U.G students), one boys hostel (for P.G. students). The two boys hostels have the capacity to accommodate 270 students. The P.G. hostel has a capacity of 60 single rooms.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Badminton Court, Basketball Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground, Table Tennis, Volleyball

reviews.review_heading: It is a nice place to build our career.
reviews.review_text: Facilities are good. The network connection is quite poor, to be honest. We have library facilities. Our college conducts tournaments every year for all sports. Inter-college matches are available for all sports. The canteen food is below average.

reviews.review_heading: Good college and infrastructure.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure was good for a engineering college. ATM and a bank inside the college. There was a garden that contained a good space for entertainment. Labs were spacious and had a good number of pc. Classrooms were spacious and digitally updated. The hostel was good. Mess food was average. There was a cricket ground volleyball court badminton court and indoor playground available.

reviews.review_heading: Puducherry Technological University.
reviews.review_text: Hostel life is very good. The food is a little bit average because most of the dishes are south indian, but the taste is very decent and much better than the other college hostel and mess. You can request the menu change as the menu is decided by the students only.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfactory college with decent faculty members and placements.
reviews.review_text: Campus has a central Wi-Fi that works all over the campus. The campus is also very big. Library has more than 8,000 books on all streams of engineering. Classrooms are decent.

reviews.review_heading: Campus life and hostel life are good.
reviews.review_text: In the hostel, the in and out timings are okay, but the food is typical South Indian and below average. Electricity is there most of the time, but it goes off during heavy rain. Still, there is a generator for power backup. Wi-Fi facility is good, but mobile internet is not there.

reviews.review_heading: The college is good for day scholars but not for hostellers.
reviews.review_text: There is no Wi-Fi in the classrooms, but Wi-Fi in the hostel rooms is free. The hostel food is not good yet, and no canteen for hostellers. There are no medical facilities after 5 PM. Sports and games are there, but at night, there are no students on the ground after 7:00 PM, and there is no lighting facility on the ground.

reviews.review_heading: College has a good ground facility.
reviews.review_text: College has a gym facility but its access is allowed only to hostellers. It has a decent canteen facility. The campus will be open from 9:00 AM- 6:00 PM. College ground is the place I hang out the most and the locality is safe.

reviews.review_heading: In my college, the education is affordable with good value.
reviews.review_text: Every student will be given a library passbook for the purpose of borrowing books. Sports events are conducted for every sport annually for all departments and the overall champion is chosen.

reviews.review_heading: You can have memories and friends, but they don't offer proper placements.
reviews.review_text: There are no proper classrooms for both students and staff. The hostel is in the worst condition. There is no quality food and water. The building is too old. The library is a good place to read and spend some useful time in our college. The canteen is so good and for six months once we used to conduct sports event without fails.

reviews.review_heading: It is a very good college for M.Tech.
reviews.review_text: The hostel facilities were not good. There was no network for Jio users, and the Wi-Fi stopped working every once in a while. Preparation was not done quickly. The food was not great. Non-veg was provided three times a week, and sometimes the food was good, while other times it was bad. It was a little cheaper compared to staying at rented places. Restrooms were not very clean. Overall, the hostel facilities were not good.

reviews.review_heading: My honest review on the college.
reviews.review_text: The syllabus will be updated every three years for every course conducted in the university. Course curriculum will cope with the demands of the changing world. Also, for master students, there was an option for choosing soft core courses from other departments to get more credit. Hostel could provide a good environment for the students. In the hostel, four students could share one room. The meal quality was average in the hostel. The hostel cost was little expensive. Overall, the hostel facilit...

reviews.review_heading: It is a gateway to a wonderful journey and success.
reviews.review_text: Facilities provided by the college are fantastic. There is free Wi-Fi throughout the campus but in some areas, there is restricted access. Classrooms are neat and spacious. There is a good environment to study. I am a day scholar, so I do not know much about the hostel but my friends said it is very normal with average food. The availability of food in the canteen and mess is super. There are medical facilities, sports and games.

reviews.review_heading: PTU (PEC) - (Student review).
reviews.review_text: College has not done proper renovation and less maintenance services. Wi-Fi is present across each department with low connectivity. Library is outdated and some books are in bad condition. Canteen has less ventilation and decent food is available. The sports ground and equipments are present separately. There is a small complex present for students, staffs, and hostel students. There is a small dispensary and medical equipment with an ambulance.

reviews.review_heading: Still in developing stage, bad placements, bad infrastructure and facilities.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure in this college is still in the developing stage. Classrooms are not equipped with digital equipment or any Wi-Fi requirements. The library is very outdated. Labs are very old and equipped with very old equipment. The hostel is also very basic which is frequently disrupted by water and Wi-Fi.

reviews.review_heading: PEC college review by a 3rd year student .
reviews.review_text: When it comes to infrastructure, it is good that you will get decent old-fashioned classroom sets with outdated syllabi. Even the labs aren't modern; they use very old machines. Hostels are decent, but the food is not so good. There is an immediate medical team inside the campus.

From this, 799 qualified students got placed with a good median salary of Rs.452000 and 59 students got selected for higher studies. In SNS College of Technology for PG [2 Years Program(s)], 25 students admitted in year 2017-18, out of which 25 graduated in 2018-19. fees_and_eligibility.overview: SNS College of Technology, SNS Group of Institutions provides Full Time programs at the UG and PG levels. faqs.answer: A:    TANCET application fee may or may not change across exam sessions. The application fee for TANCET was revised previously. Earlier, candidates had to pay INR 400 if they belonged to reserved categories and INR 800 if they belonged to unreserved categories. However, the fee for all categories was hiked. Unreserved categories had to pay INR 1000 and INR 500, respectively. Since the exam fees was revised in the recent session, Anna University is unlikely to change the same again. However, the application details along with the TANCET registration fee is to release soon.

Hence, aspirants with 60% in Class 12 can get admission. There is a 5% relaxation in the aggregate for BC/ MBC/ SC/ ST candidates.

faqs.answer: Yes, SNS College of Technology offers two-year MBA programme spread across four semesters. Admission to MBA programme is based on TANCET score followed by university counselling. Candidates who have passed graduation with a minimum of 50% aggregate can apply for the MBA course.
faqs.question: Q: Is MBA available at SNS College of Technology?

faqs.answer: Yes, candidates seeking admission in PG or PhD courses need to pass the accepted entrance exam. Admission to MBA/ MCA is based on TANCET scores. overview_faqs.question: What is the fees range for courses offerred by the SNS College of Technology?
overview_faqs.answer: The fees range for courses offerred by the SNS College of Technology is around INR 35000 - INR 200000.

overview_faqs.question: Is MBA available at SNS College of Technology?
overview_faqs.answer: Yes, SNS College of Technology offers two-year MBA programme spread across four semesters. Admission to MBA programme is based on TANCET score followed by university counselling. Candidates who have passed graduation with a minimum of 50% aggregate can apply for the MBA course.

overview_faqs.question: What is TANCET application fee? Do reserved categories have any fee concessions?
overview_faqs.answer: TANCET application fee may or may not change across exam sessions. The application fee for TANCET was revised previously. Earlier, candidates had to pay INR 400 if they belonged to reserved categories and INR 800 if they belonged to unreserved categories. However, the fee for all categories was hiked. Unreserved categories had to pay INR 1000 and INR 500, respectively. Since the exam fees was revised in the recent session, Anna University is unlikely to change the same again. However, the application details along with the TANCET registration fee is to release soon.

overview_faqs.question: Is SNS College of Technology a good college/Institute?
overview_faqs.answer: SNS College of Technology is a well-known institution for B.E. / B.Tech, MCA, M.E./M.Tech, MBA/PGDM courses. These programs are delivered by highly experienced faculty. As per Shiksha's verified reviews, SNS College of Technology has a rating of 4.21 on the basis of Shiksha-verified reviews by students. Not just this, it has also been rated 4.38 on Campus Life, 4.08 on Placements, 4.38 on Faculty and 4.1 on Infrastructure.

individual_reviews.title: SNS College of Technology is a really good engineering college in Coimbatore.

content.Placements: SNS College of Technology is a good placement college in Coimbatore. This college has a good placement training team. They conduct more placement training classes every week. They brought more than 15 companies to the college campus for interviews, for example, Tata Motors, Tata Electronics, Yamaha, Hero, TVS, Hyundai, and Ashok Leyland. The salary packages range from 3.5-12 LPA. 95% of students are placed in reputed companies.

reviews.review_heading: Life at SNS College. We experience studies with freedom.
reviews.review_text: Our college has a wide and large campus with technology, engineering, nursing, allied science, and pharmacy, and also two separate hostels for boys and girls inside the campus. We have lawn spaces to sit, study, and enjoy during break hours. There are 2 libraries, 2 cafeterias, and one large food court. We have a large sports ground for students. Hostel has lots of cupboards and tables for each student separately. Foodcourt provides good food of good quality at a cheaper price. Food in hostel is...

reviews.review_heading: The way the structure of the college is designed bring design thinking in every nook and corner.
reviews.review_text: The hostel and classrooms have free Wi-Fi. Hostel food is quite good. There are medical and sports facilities. Games are available like basketball, badminton, volleyball, and many more. College provides a lot of labs and other facilities for students to enrich their skills.

reviews.review_heading: My Genuine review about SNS college of technology.
reviews.review_text: The whole campus supports Wi-Fi connectivity, but with very low internet speed. Here, we don't have hi-fi systems in our lab; mostly all the labs are equipped with AC. Every classroom is equipped with a projector and smart board and has proper ventilation. The hostel is situated inside the campus. Talking about the quality of food available in the mess, it is very average. There is a food court available inside the campus which provides good quality food. SNS College of Technology gives more imp...

reviews.review_heading: Insights of SNSCT - the paradise.
reviews.review_text: The basic infrastructure in my department is good, except the Wi-Fi connection is very unstable in most of the places. The quality of my hostel is very good, with all the necessary requirements. But the only drawback is the food in my hostel; it is very poor for the fees they are asking. There is also a small games room with chess and carrom in my hostel.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied with my placement, but I will not pursue more core companies.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is better than mine. And other students like mind in a different way. Wi-Fi, labs and classrooms are good. Our hostel food is normal but this time we should improve the kitchen side. And review with all staff and improve it.

reviews.review_heading: Good college with an affordable fee.
reviews.review_text: The college also focused on sports and other activities. To support this activity, the college has well-trained staff and trainers and good infrastructure for that. Our college also has good Wi-Fi and hostel facilities, and hostel food is pretty good and it's safe for girls'.

paragraphs: TANCET application fee may or may not change across exam sessions. The application fee for TANCET was revised previously. Earlier, candidates had to pay INR 400 if they belonged to reserved categories and INR 800 if they belonged to unreserved categories. However, the fee for all categories was hiked. Unreserved categories had to pay INR 1000 and INR 500, respectively. Since the exam fees was revised in the recent session, Anna University is unlikely to change the same again. However, the application details along with the TANCET registration fee is to release soon.

Yes, SNS College of Technology offers merit-based scholarships to students seeking admission under Government or Management Quota. Students need to fulfil the scholarship criteria for selection. Students can avail a maximum of two scholarships. A maximum of INR 10,000 is offered as scholarship amount per year.

Classrooms are big and well-cleaned with digital boards. A well-stocked library is present with all resources available.

reviews.review_heading: To shine in your career, choose the best here, the best Sri Manakula Vinayagar Engineering College.
reviews.review_text: About 150 acres of college with environmental amenities, and AICTE approved idea lab and library facilities. Hostel facility has been split into two, one is with AC and another without AC. Canteen food is also available in our college with good quality.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Sri Manakula Vinayagar Engineering College.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure at Sri Manakula Vinayagar Engineering College is modern and functional. The campus boasts smart classrooms, advanced laboratories, a digital library, an innovation hub and Wi-Fi-enabled classrooms. The eco-friendly architecture includes solar-powered buildings and green spaces.

reviews.review_heading: It is worth for money because the college is so good in everything but the sports are only less.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the college is so excellent. They have good infrastructure and they are building the new hostel. Classrooms and labs are in a good manner. The ideal lab of the college has a very good atmosphere. There we can get so many ideas.

reviews.review_heading: Dissatisfied because of faculty, average placements and no enjoyment.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructures are good for labs and the library, but in the classroom, it's average. They can't provide Wi-Fi for class; we have to use our personal devices. Hostels are not too good; they are moderate. Canteen food is also average, but prices are high compared to quantity and quality.

reviews.review_heading: Insights on Course Structure and Faculty in Computer Science.
reviews.review_text: Coming to infrastructure, I would say that it's below average. They provide Wi-Fi within the circumstances, and it's not good either. The classrooms are good. Coming to labs, it's very well-equipped with updated systems, and the library is provided with more than 10,000 books of different genres.

reviews.review_heading: I almost like all faculty member.
reviews.review_text: We have more holidays as festivals but we have only festival in college means. It is cultural basically cultural is very nice in my college. The range is nearly 1 km from the entrance and accessibility of books is nearly 1 month. The faculties and technological provisions are very good. Mostly our College support extracurricular activities more. In our college no clubs and no social groups are formed.

reviews.review_heading: we go under the technology and learn.
reviews.review_text: We have more holidays as festivals but we have only festival in college means it is cultural basically. Cultural is very nice in my college. The range is nearly 1 km from the entrance and accessibility of books is nearly 1 month the faculties. Technological provisions are very good. Mostly our college support extracurricular activities more. In our college no clubs and no social groups are formed.

reviews.review_heading: Our college provides good facilities and placements.
reviews.review_text: The college has good infrastructure with good lab facilities. There are high-quality computer labs and clean classrooms. A library is available for the students in the AI & DS block. We also have a canteen with some snacks. There are sports like basketball, cricket, and badminton. Students can join NCC for a good career.

reviews.review_heading: Faculty are too good. They will give you better understanding of the concepts.
reviews.review_text: Hostel according to me deserves a one-star. The rooms are small enough to accommodate only one person, however, two need to stay in one room. There is no AC availability here. If you need AC you have to pay 75,000 extra per year for it which is completely absurd. Hostel mess food is also not that great. There has been insects everywhere in the food time and again and we have complained about it multiple times, but no action has been taken. So in my opinion, paying 200000 for this hostel is not a...

reviews.review_heading: Don't waste your life by joining this college.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of my college is excellent, but sometimes there are many damages in the roads and classrooms, which get worse when it rains. We have libraries, but they don't have a period for reading. If you are interested, you can go to the admin office, get a library pass, and use the library. Other than that, the canteen service is not as good as you might think; it feels like a scam, so choose wisely.

reviews.review_heading: Nice college which is student friendly.
reviews.review_text: There were many such facilities in the college, such as Wi-Fi access that was available. We had classrooms in all the classes, and the labs were well managed and organized with good quality chairs. There were all kinds of ways we could get hands-on experience. I don't live in a hostel because I live in my house.

reviews.review_heading: Strict college but strictness and discipline makes you stronger.
reviews.review_text: College infrastructure is inspired by Western architecture and Portuguese architecture. The college environment is very strict; boys and girls can not talk on campus. The college library is good; it is open until 9 p.m. and six days a week. There is no proper drinking water facility on campus; drinking water comes in cans. The hostel fee is 60,000 plus a 5,000 deposit. Mess food is there but frankly, mess food is not good and not hygienic. There is no big canteen on campus, so we can not eat whe...

reviews.review_heading: The review about my college.
reviews.review_text: My college has good infrastructure and a peaceful environment. They have separate buildings for each stream, like engineering, arts and sciences, law and so on. The labs are good but the equipment is a little bit old and the classrooms are simple and don't have projectors in them. In a library, you can't access all the books in it and its tough to take books in a library. About hostels, they have separate hostels for boys and girls but girls hostels are at medical colleges, and they have two bui...

reviews.review_heading: I got new friends and good staffs in this college.
reviews.review_text: Hostel Facilities: The hostel facilities are in good condition and the improvements to the hostel are not a must. The rooms are in good condition, the hostel food is very good and the registration process for the hostel and affordability are given in the admission block.

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Sri Venkateswara College of Engineering, Tirupati offers diverse programmes at UG and PG levels in Full Time mode. The offered programmes can be chosen from streams such as Engineering, Business & Management Studies and IT & Software. At the UG level, candidates have the provision to opt from courses such as B.E. / B.Tech. Whereas, at PG level, Sri Venkateswara College of Engineering, Tirupati offers courses such as M.E./M.Tech, MBA/PGDM and MCA. SVCE Tirupati fee structure for all the courses is around INR 70,000 to 2,98,000.

faqs.answer: A:     Sri Venkateswara College of Engineering, Tirupati has hostel facilities for students. faqs.answer: A:    Sri Venkateswara College of Engineering, Tirupati offers MBA and MCA specialisations. In 2024, the Andhra Pradesh ICET cutoff had been released by the Institute for Home State categories. The College of Arts and Commerce cutoff 2024 for Master of Computer Applications (MCA) was 17660, and for MBA in Retail Management, it stood at 34430 for the General Home State quota. So, considering the Sri Venkateswara College of Engineering, Tirupati cutoff 2024, we can assume that to get into the institute, candidates will have to acquire a rank less than 20000 for MBA admissions. Candidate must have also qualified GATE/AP-PGECET Entrance Tests.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Civil Engineering - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: Totally good at all equipment and I am really satisfied in this college.

content.Placements: I am from the 2023–2027 B. Tech batch, and overall the placement situation in our college is very strong. A number of companies visit the campus, and many of them prefer hiring male and female candidates. Opportunities are good. The placement faculty puts in good effort and regularly shares off-campus and pooled drive information. Around 40–50% of students from my course got placed. The highest package was around ₹6 LPA, the lowest about ₹2.5 LPA, and the average package stayed near ₹3–3.2 LPA. Companies like construction companies and other companies. Internships were also high.

faqs.question: Is MU - Mewar University good for placements?
faqs.answer: Located in Chittorgarh, Rajasthan, Mewar University boasts a student-centric placement cell dedicated to aiding students in securing their desired employment opportunities. As per the latest data, the university has successfully placed over 600 students with an impressive average package of Rs. 4.2 LPA. Notable recruiters in 2023 included prominent names such as Wipro, TCS, Genpact, Lupin, ICICI Bank, and CEAT Tyres. The placement process involves essential steps like registering on the Campus Interaction Portal, engaging with recruiters for campus placements, completing the Employer Registration Form (ERF), and collaboratively determining dates for pre-placement talks and final placements. While the available information suggests a favorable placement record for Mewar University, it is advisable to conduct additional research and seek insights from alumni or current students for a comprehensive evaluation before making any decisions.

reviews.review_heading: Good college with quality teaching.
reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure available for my course and department are good. Wi-Fi is not provided to students. Labs and classrooms are spacious and good. The library is big, and there are many books available for my course. Hostel facilities and the canteen are average.

reviews.review_heading: Very good college in our city
reviews.review_text: The facilities and infrastructure in our college are very good. We have good Wi-Fi connections, very new computers, and very good library rooms. The hostel food is very hygienic, and the food is also good. The rooms are very clean, and there are good sports and games.

reviews.review_heading: Good learning environment with decent placements, strong academics.
reviews.review_text: Our department has Wi-Fi, equipped labs, smart classrooms, and a good library. Hostel facilities are decent with clean rooms, normal mess food, canteen snacks, basic medical support, and good sports grounds for games and fitness. Good environment.

reviews.review_heading: Very satisfied and good.
reviews.review_text: The ECE department has well-developed academic and technical facilities to support both theory and hands-on learning. ECE has fully equipped labs. These labs have oscilloscopes, signal generators, a trainer kit, sports, and monthly events are conducted.

reviews.review_heading: The ECE course is best, and my faculty is well-experienced and very friendly with students.
reviews.review_text: The facilities provided in hostels are well-equipped. The rooms are cleaned every day. The canteen and mess food is very good and tasty.

reviews.review_heading: Very satisfied with the industry-relevant IT course with good labs and supportive staff.
reviews.review_text: Internet speed is usually stable enough for coding, online labs, and project submissions. Labs are maintained regularly and have air-conditioning in most campuses. Many colleges provide smart classrooms with projectors, audio systems, and digital boards.

reviews.review_heading: It is a very good and useful college.
reviews.review_text: Super and good facilities and infrastructure in our college. We have good speed Wi-Fi connections in our block and super-fast computer systems in labs. Library books are also good, and the facilities in our college are nice. Food is good, and the rooms are hygienic.

reviews.review_heading: Very nice and beautiful college.
reviews.review_text: The facilities in our college are above average, and the infrastructure of our college/department is very good. We have very high-speed internet, good labs, updated systems, large classrooms, and good hostel rooms. Additionally, we have hygienic food.

reviews.review_heading: Good placements and practical training in Cyber Security.
reviews.review_text: Our Cyber Security course had well-equipped labs with updated tools, high-speed Wi-Fi, smart classrooms, and access to a good digital library. The hostel provided clean rooms, continuous water supply, and decent security. Mess food is average but hygienic and the canteen offered better options. Basic medical facilities are available on campus along with playgrounds, indoor games, and areas for fitness and recreation creating a comfortable overall living and learning environment.

fees_and_eligibility.overview: The modes of PSNA College of Engineering and Technology admission are online and offline. Admission to various courses is based on merit and entrance exam scores. At the UG level, the college offers three to four-year BE courses to students. At the PG level, the college offers two-year ME, MBA, and MCA courses to students. M.E/ MTech is offered in four specialisations. Below are some of the courses offered by PSNA College of Engineering and Technology and their respective eligibility and fee:

faqs.answer: A:    PSNA College of Engineering and Technology admission 2025 is primarily based on entrance exam. The college accepts state-level entrance exams like TANCET MBA, etc. for admission to specific courses. PSNA College of Engineering and Technology offers 18 courses in affiliation with Anna University at UG and PG levels in multiple streams like Engineering, Management and Computer Applications streams.PSNA College of Engineering & Technology (PSNACET) was established in 1984. It is a premier institution at the outskirts of Dindigul Township in Tamil Nadu. The institute offers 7 undergraduate courses (B.E/B.Tech) and 8 postgraduate courses in Engineering. It also offers two-year MBA and MCA programs. PSNACET is affiliated to Anna University. Applications for admissions to all the courses have to be submitted online.Candidates must have passed 10+2 in the Science stream for admission to 4-year B.E courses.Applicants who hold Bachelor’s degree are eligible to apply for the PG courses.Admissions to B.E programme will be conducted through the admission process as per the guidelines of Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA).For admission to M.E and MBA courses, the candidates have to qualify Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET), conducted by Anna University.This is the criteria  to get admission.
faqs.question: Which entrance exam scores are accepted by PSNA College of Engineering and Technology for admission?

faqs.answer: A:    For B.Tech admissions, PSNA College of Engineering and Technology mostly takes state-level entrance exams such as TNEA. Accepting JEE scores for admission to the B.Tech programme is not mentioned specifically. The Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA) exam is used for B.Tech admissions, and the college's admissions requirements are entrance-based.Consequently, PSNA College of Engineering and Technology uses the TNEA exam as the primary basis for B.Tech admissions, while the college may take other entrance exams into consideration for particular fields.
faqs.question: Does PSNA College of Engineering and Technology accept JEE score?

faqs.answer: A:    The selection to MBA is based on TANCET score. The Government Quota seats are filled through TANCET while Management Quota seats are filled through Consortium self-finance colleges. After completion of the selection rounds, the university releases a merit list to announce the names of the shortlisted candidates. The shortlisted aspirants are called for counselling and document verification. Aspirants need to pay a course fee to confirm their seat in the college.
faqs.question: Is entrance exam required for MBA admission at PSNA College of Engineering & Technology?

faqs.answer: A:    Students are only accepted into the institute if they have successfully completed their Bachelors with a minimum aggregate of 45%. As for the 2023 cutoff for MBA at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology, the minimum marks to be scored in TANCET in the fields of MBA Finance, MBA Human Resource Management, MBA Information Technology and MBA Marketing is 43. TANCET is a state level entrance examination conducted for admission for the courses of MBA and MCA inside Tamil Nadu. The maximum mark that can be scored in this examination is 100.

faqs.answer: The college offers 18 courses in UG and PG levels in multiple streams like Engineering, Management and Computer Applications streams. The top courses offered at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology are Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, and Electrical and Electronics Engineering. Candidates who have qualified for Class 12 can apply for the UG courses and graduation for PG courses. Admission is primarily entrance-based for all courses.
faqs.question: Q: What are the top courses offered at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology?

faqs.answer: Admission to PSNA is a multi-step process. Candidates can check below the steps to apply for PSNACET:Application: The mode of application is online. Candidates can register on the official website of the college.Selection Rounds: Candidates need to qualify TANCET for admission to MBA, MCA and ME courses. For BE/ BTech admission is based on merit in the last qualifying exam.Counselling & Admission Confirmation: After completion of the selection process, candidates will need to come for TNEA Counselling for BE/ BTech admission. For all other courses, the college will conduct counselling and document verification round.
faqs.question: Q: What is the admission process at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology?

faqs.answer: PSNA College of Engineering and Technology admission 2025 is primarily based on entrance exam. The college accepts state-level entrance exams like TANCET MBA, etc. for admission to specific courses. PSNA College of Engineering and Technology offers 18 courses in affiliation with Anna University at UG and PG levels in multiple streams like Engineering, Management and Computer Applications streams.PSNA College of Engineering & Technology (PSNACET) was established in 1984. It is a premier institution at the outskirts of Dindigul Township in Tamil Nadu. The institute offers 7 undergraduate courses (B.E/B.Tech) and 8 postgraduate courses in Engineering. It also offers two-year MBA and MCA programs. PSNACET is affiliated to Anna University. Applications for admissions to all the courses have to be submitted online.Candidates must have passed 10+2 in the Science stream for admission to 4-year B.E courses.Applicants who hold Bachelor’s degree are eligible to apply for the PG courses.Admissions to B.E programme will be conducted through the admission process as per the guidelines of Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA).For admission to M.E and MBA courses, the candidates have to qualify Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET), conducted by Anna University.This is the criteria to get admission.
faqs.question: Q: Which entrance exam scores are accepted by PSNA College of Engineering and Technology for admission?

faqs.answer: For B.Tech admissions, PSNA College of Engineering and Technology mostly takes state-level entrance exams such as TNEA. Accepting JEE scores for admission to the B.Tech programme is not mentioned specifically. The Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA) exam is used for B.Tech admissions, and the college's admissions requirements are entrance-based.Consequently, PSNA College of Engineering and Technology uses the TNEA exam as the primary basis for B.Tech admissions, while the college may take other entrance exams into consideration for particular fields.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Information Technology - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Supportive campus with good placements, strong scholarships and student life.

content.Placements: Around 80% of students got placed through campus placements. The average salary is 4.25 LPA. The highest salary is 11 LPA, and the lowest is around 2.5 LPA. Top companies like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, and Capgemini came for placements. About 60% of students got internships in IT companies and startups. Jobs offered include Software Developer Trainee, System Engineer, Analyst, and support roles.
content.Infrastructure: The college has decent infrastructure that helps learning. Classrooms are spacious, labs are useful, Wi-Fi works well, and the library is well-maintained. Hostel facilities are safe and comfortable. The food is hygienic, medical help is available, and sports facilities support both indoor and outdoor games for a balanced student life.

admission_process.title: PSNA College of Engineering and Technology Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: PSNA College of Engineering and Technology admission 2025is primarily based on theentrance exam. The college accepts state-level entrance exams such as TANCET for MBA and MTech, and TNEA for admission to the BTech programme.PSNA College of Engineering and Technologyoffers four courses in affiliation withAnna Universityat UG and PG levels in multiple streams like Engineering, Management and Computer Applications streams. Interested candidates must meet the eligibility criteria before applying to their desired course.

PSNA College of Engineering and Technology courses are available at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. The number of courses offered is four and they are offered in the field of Engineering, Management and Computer Applications. Candidates seeking admission atPSNA College of Engineering and Technologyare required to meet the eligibility criteria of their desired course before applying. The table below shows the eligibility and selection criteria of thePSNA College of Engineering and Technology courses:
Bachelor's degree with at least 50% aggregate (45% aggregate for reserved category)

PSNA College of Engineering and Technology admission process is a multi-step process that begins with the release of application forms followed by shortlisting candidates based on the course-specific admission criteria and then acceptance of the admission offer by the candidates.
The step-wisePSNA College of Engineering admission processis given below:
Step 1: Application process:

Candidates seeking admission at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology, are required to apply for their desired course after meeting the course-specific eligibility criteria. The aspirants need to do so before the stipulated date and pay the required application fee. Students can also check the detailedPSNA College of Engineering and Technology feestructure here.
Step 2: Selection Rounds:

To shortlist candidates, the college accepts state-level entrance exams such as TANCET for MBA and MTech, and TNEA for admission to the BTech programme. Candidates must perform well in the entrance exams to get shortlisted for admission at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology.
Step 3: Final Admission:
The selected candidates will have to confirm their admission by paying the admission confirmation fee and visiting the college campus for the document verification process.

faqs.question: Does PSNA College of Engineering and Technology accept JEE score?
faqs.answer: PSNA College of Engineering and Technology does not rely on JEE scores for admission to its B.Tech programs. Instead, the college's admission process for B.Tech is conducted through the Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA), which is overseen by Anna University, Chennai. This indicates that aspiring students seeking admission to B.Tech programs at PSNA College are required to participate in the TNEA process, which involves meeting specific eligibility criteria and adhering to the guidelines set forth by the university.For prospective applicants interested in pursuing B.Tech studies at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology, it is advisable to visit the official website of the college or directly contact the admissions office to obtain detailed information about the admission process, eligibility criteria, important dates, and any other relevant requirements. By consulting official sources, aspiring students can gain clarity on the specific procedures and prerequisites involved i

faqs.question: Is TNEA Counselling required for BTech admission at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology?
faqs.answer: Yes, TNEA Counselling is required for BTech admission. candidates who have completed Class 12 with 50% each in Physics and Chemistry and 100% in Mathematics are eligible to apply for BTech and BE courses. Aspirants are shortlisted based on their merit in the last qualifying exam. The shortlisted candidates need to come for TNEA Counselling for final selection.

faqs.question: What is the admission process at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology?
faqs.answer: Admission to PSNA is a multi-step process. Candidates can check below the steps to apply for PSNACET:Application:The mode of application is online. Candidates can register on the official website of the college.Selection Rounds:Candidates need to qualify TANCET for admission to MBA, MCA and ME courses. For BE/ BTech admission is based on merit in the last qualifying exam.Counselling & Admission Confirmation:After completion of the selection process, candidates will need to come for TNEA Counselling for BE/ BTech admission. For all other courses, the college will conduct counselling and document verification round.

faqs.question: What is the latest cutoff information for MBA at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology?
faqs.answer: Students are only accepted into the institute if they have successfully completed their Bachelors with a minimum aggregate of 45%. As for the 2023 cutoff for MBA at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology, the minimum marks to be scored in TANCET in the fields of MBA Finance, MBA Human Resource Management, MBA Information Technology and MBA Marketing is 43. TANCET is a state level entrance examination conducted for admission for the courses of MBA and MCA inside Tamil Nadu. The maximum mark that can be scored in this examination is 100.

paragraphs: The PSNA College of Engineering and Technology placement report recorded a highest package of INR 44 LPA in 2025. The average package stood at INR 4.2 LPA . A total of 104 recruiters took part in the recruitment process and made more than 1107 job offers. A total of 1039 students got placed from various courses. The season saw a total of 18 offers above INR 12 LPA, 58 offers between INR 5-10 LPA, 385 offers between INR 4-5 LPA and 263 offers between INR 3-4 LPA. Leading recruiters include-ICICI, HCL, Cognizant and others taking part in the PSNA College of Engineering and Technology placement season 2025.

description: PSNACET cutoff 2025 for admission to the BE/BTech program at the undergraduate level has been released. The cutoff list is out for the General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, and PWD categories. Candidates can check out the exam-wise section below to get an idea of the PSNACET cutoff 2025 across different exams, courses, rounds, and categories. Further updates are provided below:

PSNACET TANCET Cutoff 2025 is out for admission to MBA/PGDM for all rounds and categories for students belonging to the AI quota. The maximum TANCET cutoff required for admissions in the last round was 3373 for MBA/PGDM admission in the General category. Tabulated below is the TANCET first and last round cutoff 2025.

PSNACET MBA/PGDM cutoff 2025 can be seen in this section, along with the previous 2-years cutoff data, i.e., 2024, and 2023. In 2025 the last round rank for MBA/PGDM stood at 3373 among the students belonging to the General AI quota. For the OBC AI quota, the cutoff rank for the same course was 3888.
After comparing both the categories, the last Round cutoff rank for General AI category students was more competitive for admission than the OBC AI students.

PSNACET accepts the TANCET cutoff for admissions to various specialisation of MBA/PGDM. Students who are interested in admissions at this institute can refer to the table below to know the important dates regarding registration, exam, etc.

TANCET 2025 cutoff for PSNACET is available in the table below, along with last two years cutoff data.The cutoff has been released for all rounds and categories. Students preparing for admission can download the consollidated round and year-wise cutoff data to understand the cutoff trends.

Students can view the PSNACET BTech cutoff for 2025, as well as the cutoff details for 2024 and 2023 across various rounds, categories, and branches. For the General AI category, the most competitive branch for admission during the last round at PSNACET was B.E. in Computer Science and Engineering, with a cutoff rank of 29113.

hostel_campus.college_name: PSNA College of Engineering and Technology Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description: PSNACET has 8 hostel blocks which include 5Gents Hostels, 2 Ladies Hostel, and 1 PIO-Foreign Students Hostel. All the hostels are offered RO drinking water facility, health care facilities, indoor and outdoor games facilities and other facilities like STD booth, photocopying, stationery and general store.

library.description: PSNA College of Engineering and Technology has a central library, in addition to it, there are departmental libraries in each department. The library has 91370 volumes of books on 39298 titles, 5583 back volumes of journals/ magazine, 10274 nonbook materials. It also has the subscription of more than 2628 scholarly journals under the AICTE INDEST Consortium and 124 print versions of the journals. The library provides On-line access to a large number of full-text journals from IEEE, ASME, ASCE, Elsevier, and J-Gate (JSMS). The library has the institutional membership 	British Council Library, American Information Resource Center etc.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Badminton Court, Basketball Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground, Tennis Court, Hockey Ground, Kabaddi Ground, Volleyball Ground, Track

cafeteria.description: College cafeteria has three spacious sections separately for men students, women students, faculty, and visitors. These three sections together can accommodate 500 members at a time. In addition to this cafeteria, three tea/coffee shops are located on the campus.

reviews.review_heading: Supportive campus with good placements, strong scholarships and student life.
reviews.review_text: The college has decent infrastructure that helps learning. Classrooms are spacious, labs are useful, Wi-Fi works well, and the library is well-maintained. Hostel facilities are safe and comfortable. The food is hygienic, medical help is available, and sports facilities support both indoor and outdoor games for a balanced student life.

reviews.review_heading: A good engineering college, and it will be a value-for-money education.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms and library was good, and on the campus, free Wi-Fi facilities are available. In labs, the systems are all very good. Separate labs are given for each department, and the labs are very comfortable with AC, neat and clean.

reviews.review_heading: A good place for the right path of career education.
reviews.review_text: The college hostel infrastructure is really good. The lab facilities and Wi-Fi facilities are all available in our college. In our library, free Wi-Fi is available, and the food will be of such quality and hygienic that there will be veg and non-veg provided in the mess and also the canteen.

reviews.review_heading: Teachers are friendly. It provides good placements.
reviews.review_text: The college infrastructure is beautiful. Classrooms are also good. Labs are well-equipped with air conditioning. It has high-speed internet connectivity. Many technical and financial books are available in the library. The hostel facilities are good with an attached bathroom. The canteen is also available. Medical facilities are good.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely best and great college.
reviews.review_text: Our college infrastructure was good. The classrooms were neat with comfortable benches and sufficient labs. Many technical and financial books were available in the library. Wi-Fi facility was also available. Hostel facilities were good. The canteen was also available. Medical facilities were available.

reviews.review_heading: PSNACET - A hub for quality engineering education and career growth.
reviews.review_text: Computer science and engineering (CSE) department at PSNA College of Engineering and Technology (PSNACET) is equipped with state-of-the-art infrastructure to support high-quality technical education and research. The department features modern classrooms with smart boards and digital teaching tools, promoting interactive learning. It houses several advanced computer laboratories with high-speed internet, the latest software, and high-performance systems to support programming, artificial intelli...

reviews.review_heading: PSNA College of Engineering and Technology has the best placements and infrastructure.
reviews.review_text: This college has a well-maintained campus. More classrooms and modern laboratories are available in this college. The biggest library facilities are also there in this college. This college provides good hostel facilities for boys and girls. Hostel rooms are daily maintained very cleanly. Hostel food taste is also good. Sports grounds and coaching staff facilities are also available in this college.

reviews.review_heading: A very good college if you are searching for the best engineering college in Tamil Nadu.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is available for the students and classrooms are neat and clean. The entire infrastructure of the college is green. It's such a peaceful environment where we can study peacefully. The hostel and mess foods are too good. We also have a big playground for sports and a lot of facilities available for sports and games.

reviews.review_heading: Overall, without expectations, students can enjoy their college life if they make good friends.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms are designed for the convenience of students with wide space and projectors for easy or smart learning, The auditorium is built with centralised air-conditioner and has comfortable chairs with proper lighting, The library has a wide variety of books.

reviews.review_heading: Review of PSNA College of Engineering and Technology.
reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure available for my course are good. It provides Wi-Fi. Classrooms include library and labs. Mess and canteen facilities are available. Sports and games facilities are also available. The food quality is good.

reviews.review_heading: College you will never regret.
reviews.review_text: In my college, they provide sufficient room for the hostels and games like football court, tennis court, shuttle court, and they also conduct game competitions and others. They provide healthy food for the hostel, and it is the safest place for girls to stay who are coming from long distances.

reviews.review_heading: Good college and well-maintained.
reviews.review_text: The college infrastructure is good. Everyone can use the internet. It has a huge central library, and classrooms are good, clean, and hygienic. The labs are well-equipped. The hostel facility for both boys and girls. Our college has a canteen with some snacks. The medical facilities also available.

reviews.review_heading: Review of PSNA College of Engineering and Technology.
reviews.review_text: The Wi-Fi is present and installed everywhere except some places. The Wi-Fi password is kept secret to this date, and only the faculty members and the staff are allowed to use it, but no worries because the network is damn slow and not available inside the classrooms, only available in the lab for the faculty members only. Many of the lab systems are not capable of running a C program. Hostel food is below average; the taste of the hostel food and the canteen foods and drinks is getting even wor...

reviews.review_heading: Not worth it if you are not interested in self-learning.
reviews.review_text: There is a rare allowance for using the institution's Wi-Fi, and it is very slow. The mess and canteen are more expensive compared to the city market, and some dishes are not worth it. The service is also poor in the main canteen. The lab systems are very slow, even for basic working and functions such as browsing and system administration operations. The systems in the DSP lab are not capable.

reviews.review_heading: Fees are high, but the quality is good.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is below average. There is no library. Classrooms conditions are the worst, with only dim lighting, and sometimes mosquitoes bite us. Wi-Fi is not available. College does not have a hostel. Labs are in very bad condition, and the toilets are also poorly maintained.
